title: Renewed Strikes Threaten Setback to Shipping Recovery in Persian Gulf
summary: The attacks came after traffic through the Strait of Hormuz had reached the highest levels since the start of the U.S. war in Iran.

title: After New York Elections, Israelis Fear Further Rupture in U.S.-Israeli Relationship
summary: Criticism of Israel’s war in Gaza, anger over the Iran war and election results in New York all suggest Israel’s solid support from Washington may be on borrowed time.

title: In Small Step, Israel Agrees to Withdrawal From Two Areas in Lebanon
summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio acknowledged that the agreement marked only “the beginning of the beginning” of negotiations between Israel and Lebanon.

title: Venezuela’s Economy Was On the Rise. Then the Earthquakes Struck.
summary: Expectations are now rising over the role the United States will play in helping Venezuela, which had already been laid low by decades of misrule.

title: How the Reflecting Pool Turned Green: Missing ‘Bubblers’ and a Rush Job
summary: Bulky “nanobubbler” machines were carted off ahead of a promotional event for President Trump’s Ultimate Fighting Championship birthday party.

title: Watergate Today as a Blip of a Story? Vance Might Not Be Entirely Wrong.
summary: Nixon’s offenses were not a deep-state invention. But in the age of Donald Trump, and in a fractured media environment, voters have become more inured to political scandal.

title: Volunteers Are Risking Their Lives to Stop Ebola. They Aren’t Always Welcome.
summary: Frontline Congolese workers who conduct safe burials are key to containing what experts fear could become the worst ever Ebola outbreak. They risk not just infection, but the anger of terrified communities.

title: Riding Into 2028: On the Midterms Trail, Andy Beshear Eyes a Bigger Prize
summary: The two-term Democratic governor of deep-red Kentucky, in demand as a surrogate in key 2026 races, talks horses, faith and family politics as he considers a run for the White House.

title: Jeffries Faces Steep Test as Far Left Builds Strength in Congress
summary: The victories of Darializa Avila Chevalier and other anti-establishment candidates are changing the face of House Democrats, posing a challenge for their leader.

title: Colorado Supreme Court Delay Threatens Democratic Redistricting Effort
summary: Both parties are awaiting a decision on the validity of ballot proposals to redraw Colorado’s maps ahead of 2028. Democrats say each day without a ruling puts the proposals at risk.

title: What to Watch in Louisiana’s Republican Senate Runoff
summary: Saturday’s election will be the latest test of President Trump’s influence in G.O.P. contests.

title: Mexican Officials Have Become Informants for the Trump Administration
summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum of Mexico has pushed back against U.S. investigations into Mexican politicians. Now some politicians want to cooperate.

title: Robby Hoffman Will Always Feel Poor, No Matter How Rich She Gets
summary: The comedian and actor says class and the way she grew up inform everything about the way she lives now.

title: Trump Is Turning Journalists Into Criminals
summary: The product of journalism enjoys substantial protection under the First Amendment. But what about the process?

title: Three Opinion Writers on Trump’s Latest Face-plant
summary: Trump planned a big party for America. It’s not going well.

title: Natural Selection
summary: Sometimes, our preference for certitude, our need to define things with conviction, closes us off from possibility.

title: Pete Buttigieg Says He Was Separated From His Children in ‘Swatting’ Attack
summary: The former transportation secretary recounted being kept away from his 4-year-old twins overnight after an anonymous report accused him of posing a threat to them. The police said the report was false.
